established_patterns.context_propagation: cmp.Or(ctx.Ctx, context.Background()) at actionpins.go:317. Background ONLY at top boundaries
established_patterns.init_function_criteria: OK: analyzer flag reg, wasm build-tag override, embedded JSON, map derivation. NOT: log-only stubs
established_patterns.mutex_unlock: defer mu.Unlock dominant ~85%. Manual ok for release-before-slow-call
established_patterns.syscall_discard_defensibility: Defensible IFF (a) callee logs internally, (b) explicit best-effort doc, (c) error-cleanup defer with primary err, (d) impossible-in-practice at init. #aw_sg14a1 sites meet NONE
established_patterns.implicit_defer_discard_antipattern: `defer fn(args)` returning error implicitly discards. Worse than `_ =`. Fix: `defer func() { _ = fn(args) }()`
established_patterns.errors_is_idiom: Use errors.Is even for stdlib-unwrapped sentinels (context.Canceled, io.EOF) for project consistency. R16: 100% adoption
established_patterns.build_tag_duplicate_antipattern: Pure-value declarations duplicated across `!js,!wasm` vs `js||wasm` should hoist to untagged file. Example: ErrNpmNotAvailable→npm_validation_errors.go (R16)
established_patterns.iife_initializer_legitimate: `var X = func() T { ... }()` (trailing invocation) is OK — stores result, not function. Examples: pkg/constants/tool_constants.go:72,102; pkg/workflow/{bot_aliases.go:10, template_injection_utils.go:50, mcp_config_validation.go:49, permissions.go:11}
established_patterns.test_seam_function_var_antipattern: `var X = func(...) (...) { return realFn(...) }` (stores function value) is anti-pattern. Identifiers: (1) docstring 'can be replaced in tests', (2) test boilerplate `orig:=X; defer{X=orig}`, (3) body forwards to real same-pkg func. Fix: deps struct (CompileConfig model). 7 sites in #aw_sg16a1

bash_policy_limits: Shell blocks multi-statement, mkdir/ls/find outside /home/runner/work/gh-aw/gh-aw, awk on /dev/null. `go build` blocked. gh CLI 403 on /meta. Use Write tool over heredoc append. Compound `mkdir && ls` split-checked. >> redirect to /tmp/gh-aw/repo-memory blocked; use Write. Glob/Read on /tmp WORKS even when bash mkdir/ls blocked.
